2018-09-25.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani of Iran said Monday he would consider new talks with Washington only if President Trump reversed himself and honored the 2015 nuclear accord.

2018-09-25.title: 1893: Fatal Dynamite Outrage in Spain
2018-09-25.abstract: An anarchist attempted to kill an army marshal and former prime minister of Spain with two bombs. The explosion killed a policeman and passer-by instead.

2018-09-25.title: The Newest Curse of the Mummy: Bad Drainage
2018-09-25.abstract: Engineers and archaeologists working together to save an ancient riverside temple in Egypt have discovered rare treasures, including a sphinx.

2018-09-25.title: U.N. General Assembly Updates: Trump Speaks Out on Globalization
2018-09-25.abstract: Leaders from around the world have gathered in New York to deal with a host of nettlesome issues, ranging from Iran’s nuclear program to North Korea to wars in the Middle East.

2018-09-26.title: Deliberate or Not, Trump’s Speech Draws Laughter at the U.N.
2018-09-26.abstract: President Trump’s unscripted moment, when his bragging elicited laughter at the General Assembly, became the defining moment of his speech.

2018-09-26.title: A Marine’s Son Takes On U.S. Military Bases in Okinawa
2018-09-26.abstract: “My father is American and my mother is Okinawan,” Denny Tamaki tells voters on the Japanese islands where he is running for governor.

2018-09-26.title: Mexican Authorities Disarm Acapulco Police Amid Corruption Inquiry
2018-09-26.abstract: The federal and state police, backed by the army, took over security in the resort city, where the local force is suspected to have been infiltrated by organized crime.

2018-09-26.title: A Homage to Chinese Communism Is Destroyed in Taiwan
2018-09-26.abstract: A man’s decision to turn part of a Buddhist temple into a shrine to China’s Communist Party stirred outrage in Taiwan.

2018-09-26.title: Swedish Skit Mocks Chinese Tourists, Drawing Backlash in China
2018-09-26.abstract: Chinese officials demanded an apology over a Swedish satirical program’s skit about Chinese tourists, which the network says was meant to highlight racism.

2018-09-27.title: 383,000: Estimated Death Toll in South Sudan’s War
2018-09-27.abstract: The new report offers the first comprehensive tally of the scale of devastation in a largely forgotten war.

2018-09-27.title: Sounding Out The New York Times
2018-09-27.abstract: This week’s Australia Letter brings the world to your ears, exploring how journalism is bending toward creativity.

2018-09-27.title: Don’t You Hate When a Seal Slaps You in the Face With an Octopus?
2018-09-27.abstract: Kyle Mulinder does. As he kayaked in New Zealand, the fateful moment was captured on video.

2018-09-27.title: Japan’s Embrace of Bilateral Trade Talks With U.S. Spares It From Tariffs
2018-09-27.abstract: Japan may have given President Trump a chance to declare victory, but in the short term it has escaped damaging tariffs on its auto industry.

2018-09-27.title: U.N. General Assembly: Criticism and Praise for Trump Administration
2018-09-27.abstract: World leaders gathered in New York for the United Nations General Assembly, after two days dominated by President Trump.

2018-09-28.title: Israeli-Palestinian Divide Deepens, With No Sign of Compromise at U.N.
2018-09-28.abstract: In their General Assembly speeches, the Palestinian and Israeli leaders offered starkly opposing appraisals of an intractable dispute.

2018-09-28.title: Their Plane Was Set to Land. The Water Rushed In. Then, the Boats Came.
2018-09-28.abstract: Residents in small boats rushed to the crash site to save passengers and crew members from the sinking aircraft, an official said.

2018-09-28.title: Journalist Who Spread Conspiracy Theories Will Oversee Italy’s State TV
2018-09-28.abstract: The appointment of Marcello Foa has raised alarms and marks a victory of the populist parties over the establishment media that once discounted them.

2018-09-28.title: For Xi Jinping, Being a Man of the People Means Looking the Part
2018-09-28.abstract: A tour of farms and factories in China’s northeast gave Mr. Xi a chance to look like Mao and take aim at Trump.

2018-09-28.title: In Hong Kong, Hepatitis E Strain Jumps From Rats to Humans
2018-09-28.abstract: Researchers stressed the seriousness of even one case of animal-to-human infection in densely populated areas.

2018-09-29.title: A Prostitute Was Killed in France. Is a New Law Partly to Blame?
2018-09-29.abstract: French lawmakers passed a measure two years ago they said would make life safer for prostitutes. Some say it has put them in a lot more danger.

2018-09-29.title: Turkey Clamps Down on a Group Erdogan Once Championed: Grieving Mothers
2018-09-29.abstract: A group of mothers held weekly vigils for missing sons for years, and Recep Tayyip Erdogan once vowed to help. But the government recently banned the rallies, a sign, critics said, of rising authoritarianism.

2018-09-29.title: A Social Media Star Is Shot Dead in Baghdad. Iraqis Fear a Trend.
2018-09-29.abstract: Tara Fares was gunned down in broad daylight, the latest outspoken woman killed in a series of attacks that Iraqi officials are investigating as possibly linked.

2018-09-29.title: Pope Defrocks Fernando Karadima, Priest at Center of Abuse Outrage in Chile
2018-09-29.abstract: Francis once defended a bishop accused of covering up the case, prompting a storm of protest, but an about-face has led to a widespread reckoning in the country.

2018-09-29.title: U.S. Weighed Penalizing El Salvador Over Support for China, Then Backed Off
2018-09-29.abstract: The White House wanted to cut aid to El Salvador after it severed diplomatic ties with Taiwan in favor of China. But that could have jeopardized efforts to curb illegal immigration.

2018-09-30.title: Across Senegal, the Beloved Baobab Tree Is the ‘Pride of the Neighborhood’
2018-09-30.abstract: Baobabs have endured for centuries as essential cultural symbols. But increasingly, they are threatened by climate change, urbanization and a growing population.

2018-09-30.title: Hit Men and Power: South Africa’s Leaders Are Killing One Another
2018-09-30.abstract: About 90 politicians have been killed in South Africa since 2016. And in most cases, African National Congress officials are killing one another.

2018-09-30.title: Indonesia Tsunami Death Toll Climbs Above 800
2018-09-30.abstract: Casualty figures were expected to rise significantly as rescuers scrambled to reach the worst-hit areas. Without heavy equipment, people were using their hands to claw through rubble.

2018-09-30.title: 6 Months Before Brexit, Many in U.K. Fear ‘It’s Looking Very Grisly’
2018-09-30.abstract: In myriad ways, the country is in the same position it was on the morning after the 2016 referendum: without a clear plan.

2018-09-30.title: U.S. Marine’s Son Wins Okinawa Election on Promise to Oppose Military Base
2018-09-30.abstract: Denny Tamaki’s victory is a setback to plans to transfer a busy Marine air base to a less-populated coastal area on the island.
